# AI Video Object Removal

## Overview

**AI Video Object Removal**

![](https://plugins-media.makeupar.com/smb/blog/post/2025-02-04/webp_537ec670-48c0-49fc-b96b-8073de21a64b.webp)

The AI Video Object Removal API enables seamless removal of unwanted elements from video content. Whether dealing with crowded backgrounds filled with tourists, cluttered environments such as desks with tissues and bottles, or distracting reflections on glass surfaces, the API can precisely and reliably eliminate masked areas with high accuracy and consistency.


---

### Integration Guide

**1. Prepare a Source Video and a Mask Image**
The input video must not exceed 60 seconds in length, and the output video must have a long axis of 1920 pixels or less with a frame rate of 30 frames per second or below.


**2. Upload File**
Request upload URLs and file IDs via:

```
POST /s2s/v2.0/file/obj-rem-vid
```

Input video:

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/webp_video_object_removal_input_edd43a2470.png)


Reference input mask:

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/webp_video_object_removal_mask_4a1c9fb658.png)


**3. Execute AI Task**

```
POST /s2s/v2.0/task/obj-rem-vid
```

Submit the task using file IDs or image URLs as input. The response returns a task_id for tracking and retrieving the result.


**4. Retrieve Task Result**

```
GET /s2s/v2.0/task/obj-rem-vid/{task_id}
```

Use the task ID to track status and obtain results.

[Webhooks](/develop/webhook.md) can be configured to receive asynchronous notifications on task completion with a success or error status. Polling is also supported by repeatedly calling the task endpoint until the status is updated from running to success or error.

Usage is only charged when the task completes successfully.


Output video sample:

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/webp_video_object_removal_output_eb7670cd9e.png)


---

### File Specs & Errors

* Supported Formats & Dimensions

|AI Feature|Supported Dimensions|Supported File Size|Supported Formats|
|  ----  | ----  | ----  | ----  |
| AI Video Object Removal | The input video must not exceed 60 seconds in length, and the output video must have a long axis of 1920 pixels or less with a frame rate of 30 frames per second or below. | Length limit: 60s | container: mov, mp4 <br>video: MPEG-4, MPEG-4 AVC, <br>audio: aac, amr, mp3 |

* Error Codes

|Error Code|Description|
|  ----  | ----  |
| error_download_video | Download source video error |
| error_decode_video | Decode source video error |
| error_unsupported_video | Unsupported video format |
| exceed_max_filesize | Input file size exceeds the maximum limit|
| error_nsfw_content_detected | NSFW content detected in the source file |
| error_decode_mask | Decode mask image error |
| invalid_parameter | Invalid parameter value|

* Environment & Dependency

| Sample Code Language / Tool | Recommended Runtime Versions |
|---|---|
| cURL | - bash >= 3.2</br>   - curl >= 7.58 (modern TLS/HTTP support)</br>   - jq >= 1.6 (robust JSON parsing) |
| Node.js (JavaScript) | Node >= 18 (for global fetch) |
| JavaScript | - Chrome / Edge >= 80</br>   - Firefox >= 74</br>   - Safari >= 13.1 |
| PHP | PHP >= 7.4 (for modern TLS/compat), ext-curl (recommended) or allow_url_fopen=On + ext-openssl, ext-json |
| Python | Python >= 3.10 (for f-strings), requests >= 2.20.0 |
| Java | Java 11+ (for HttpClient), Jackson Databind >= 2.12.0 |

- **API version:** V2.0
- **OpenAPI version:** 3.0.0

## Authentication

| Name | Type | Scheme / Location | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BearerAuthenticationV2 | http | bearer | Use the standard 'Bearer authentication'. Put your 'API Key' in header: `Authorization:Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`. Notice that there is ' ' a space between 'Bearer' and the 'YOUR_API_KEY'. |

## Endpoints

### `POST` `/s2s/v2.0/file/obj-rem-vid`
**Create a new file.**

To upload a new file, you'll first need to use the File API. It will give you a URL – use that URL to upload your file. Once the upload is finished, you can use the file_id from the same response to start using our AI features.

**Tags:** V2.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Request body

**application/json** · required

**Schema:** `VideoFileV1.1`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `files` | `array<object>` | Yes | — |
| `files.content_type` | `string` | Yes | Content MIME type for this file. Currently available values are listed in the enum. |
| `files.file_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of this file |
| `files.file_size` | `integer` | Yes | Content length for this file in bytes. Should not be larger than 100MB. |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful retrieval of file upload information

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `BasicVideoFileResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `files` | `array<object>` | No | — |
| `files.content_type` | `string` | No | Content MIME type for this file. |
| `files.file_name` | `string` | No | Name of this file |
| `files.file_id` | `string` | No | ID of this file. Other run task API will need this \`file_id\`. |
| `files.requests` | `array<object>` | No | Using upload \`url\`, \`headers\`, \`method\` below to upload file. After completion, the \`file_id\` is used to proceed with calling run task API. |
| `files.requests.headers` | `object` | No | Headers to include when uploading the file |
| `files.requests.url` | `string` | No | URL to upload this file |
| `files.requests.method` | `string` | No | HTTP method to upload this file |

**400** — Invalid request parameters

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |
| `error_code` | `string` | No | — |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

### `POST` `/s2s/v2.0/task/obj-rem-vid`
**Run an AI Video Object Removal task.**

AI tasks are asynchronous. Prefer webhook-based completion handling when the feature supports webhooks. Configure your webhook endpoint, verify webhook signatures, and use the received `task_id` to query the task result after a `success` or `error` notification. See the [webhook integration guide](/develop/webhook.md) for setup and verification details.

If webhooks are not supported for the feature, or if your integration cannot use webhooks, implement polling. After starting an AI task, keep polling the task status endpoint at the given `polling_interval` until the task status is either `success` or `error`.

Do not stop polling a running task for longer than the allowed polling window. If the task is not polled in time, the task may expire; a later status check can return `InvalidTaskId` even if processing finished, and the consumed units may still be charged.

**Tags:** V2.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Request body

**application/json** · required

**Schema:** `object & BasicRunTaskV2VidSrcFileUrl & BasicRunTaskV2MskFileUrl | object & BasicRunTaskV2VidSrcFileUrl & BasicRunTaskV2MskFileId | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileId & BasicRunTaskV2MskFileUrl | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileId & BasicRunTaskV2MskFileId & object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `src_file_url` | `string` | Yes | Url of the file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `msk_file_url` | `string` | No | URL of the mask file. This should be a grey scale mask image with the exact same width and height with the input image where white pixels indicate foreground and black pixels represent background. The URL should be publicly accessible. |
| `msk_file_id` | `string` | No | ID of the mask file. This should be a grey scale mask image with the exact same width and height with the input image where white pixels indicate foreground and black pixels represent background. File ID from upload file API. |
| `src_file_id` | `string` | Yes | ID of file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `frame_idx` | `integer` | Yes | Specifies the frame index in the input video that corresponds to the provided mask image, ranging from 0 to the total frame count minus 1. |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful execution of the task

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `BasicRunTaskResponseV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `data` | `object` | No | — |
| `data.task_id` | `string` | No | ID of this task. Task result is valid to query by this ID for 24 hours. |

**400** — Failed execution of task

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `RunError`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | Error message |
| `error_code` | `string — InvalidParameters \| CreditInsufficiency \| InvalidStyleGroup \| InvalidStyle \| BadRequest` | No | Error code:   * InvalidParameters - Invalid request parameters   * CreditInsufficiency - Insufficient unit to run   * BadRequest - Unexpected request parameter   * InvalidStyleGroup - Invalid style group id   * InvalidStyle - Invalid style id |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**429** — Too many requests

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

### `GET` `/s2s/v2.0/task/obj-rem-vid/{task_id}`
**Check the status of a AI Video Object Removal task.**

**Tags:** V2.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Parameters

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `task_id` | path | `string` | Yes | ID of task to check |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful check of the task status

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `VidTaskStatusResponseBodySingleUrlResultsV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | `string` | No | URL to download this result. Valid for 2 hours |

**400** — Invalid task ID

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**500** — Task execution timeout

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |
